WHS Compliance App
Welcome to site
This app manages your daily WHS compliance — sign in, toolbox talks, pre-starts, hazard reporting and more. Setup takes about 2 minutes.
Daily sign in & out
Your attendance is recorded with time and location at the moment you sign in — not tracked at any other time.
Safety forms & records
Toolbox talks, plant pre-starts, hazard reports and incident reports — all in one place, submitted in seconds.
Safety Stars
Earn points for compliance actions and standout safety behaviour. Monthly leaderboard and prize draws.
Your data — This app collects your name, photo, GPS location at sign in/out, and form submissions for site WHS compliance purposes. Your data is stored securely in Australia, managed by Slán Safety on behalf of your employer, and never sold or shared with third parties. Location is recorded only at the moment you sign in or out.

Set up your profile

You only do this once.
After this, signing in takes two taps.

Your details

Site Supervisor

Full dashboard — see all workers, pre-starts, hazards in real time

Worker / Operator

Standard access — sign in, toolbox talk, pre-start, report hazards

Company Director

Company-wide analytics dashboard — full overview across all sites

Specific role / trade
You'll get a reminder 15 minutes before this if pre-start isn't done
Profile photo
Optional — appears in the app header
Licences & tickets
These are sourced from your induction record and training matrix. You will see them in your profile — no need to enter them here.
Signature
Sign with your finger. Stored securely on this device and applied automatically to every form.
Supervisor Access
Enter your supervisor PIN
Incorrect PIN — try again

App permissions

A few permissions are needed to keep you and your team safe.

Why we need these
This app uses your camera, location and notifications to keep you and your team safe on site. Photos and locations are stored on your company's secure systems for compliance and audit purposes, and may be accessed by an external WHS compliance service engaged by your company. By tapping Allow you consent to this use.
View full privacy policy →
Camera
Photos for permits and hazard reports
Tap to allow
Location
Sign in/out, weather, GPS-tagged photos
Tap to allow
Notifications
Permit updates, alerts, supervisor messages
Tap to allow
Privacy Policy

[Company Name] Safety App — Privacy Policy

What we collect

Camera (photos for permits and hazard reports), Location (sign in/out, weather data, GPS-tagged photos), Notifications (alerts only).

How we store it

Data is held on [Company Name]'s secure systems for the duration required by Australian workplace safety law — including audit, insurance, and incident retention requirements.

Who has access
  • Your site supervisor, project director, and authorised company personnel
  • An external WHS compliance service engaged by [Company Name] to manage safety records and reporting on their behalf
  • Regulators (e.g. SafeWork) if required by law or during an investigation
Your rights

You can request to see your data at any time by contacting your supervisor or company. Data is held for compliance and may not be deleted before retention requirements are met.

Contact

[provided by your company]

⚠️ Immediate action first
For life-threatening injuries call emergency services immediately. Make the area safe and care for anyone injured before filling out this report. Complete this form once the immediate situation is under control.
1 — Incident details
Injury — someone was hurt.
Near Miss — close call with no harm done. Rule of thumb: an everyday "could have been bad" event (e.g. tripped over a cable, dropped a bolt from a ladder).
Dangerous — more serious than Near Miss. A specific event listed in WHS Act s.37 — collapse, plant runaway, fire/explosion, hazardous release, electric shock, fall from height. These must be reported to the regulator, even with no injury.
Damage — property, equipment, vehicle or structure damaged with no injury.
Spill — environmental release: spill, contamination, breach of environmental controls.
2 — Person(s) involved
Involved — you were directly involved or were the injured person.
Witness — you saw the event happen.
Nearby — you didn't see it happen but were nearby and have relevant information (circumstantial witness).
On behalf — you're filing this for someone else who can't do it themselves.
3 — Work context
4 — What happened
5 — Immediate action & corrective action
6 — Photo evidence — optional
Take photos of the scene, equipment involved, or any visible injury (with consent). Photos help assessment without visiting site. You can skip this section if no photos are available.
No photos attached. You confirmed there are no photos available for this incident.
Add photo
7 — Is this notifiable to the regulator?
Under the WHS Act, certain incidents must be notified to SafeWork / WorkSafe immediately. This includes fatalities, serious injuries requiring hospitalisation, and dangerous incidents (near miss with potential for serious harm).
⚠️ Notifiable incident — preserve the scene.
Notifiable incidents must be reported to the regulator immediately. Do not disturb the scene apart from making it safe or assisting injured persons. Submit this report now and follow up by phone using the contact in your site contacts.
What counts as a notifiable incident?
Under WHS law, a notifiable incident is any of the following:
  • Death of any person at the workplace
  • Serious injury or illness requiring immediate hospital treatment — amputation, serious head/eye injury, serious burns, spinal injury, loss of consciousness, exposure to a hazardous substance
  • Dangerous incident that exposed anyone to a serious risk — collapse of a structure, uncontrolled spill or release, plant runaway, electric shock, fall from height with serious-harm potential
If any of these apply, treat it as notifiable. If none clearly apply, submit the report — it will be assessed and you'll be contacted within 24 hours. Take photos if it's safe to do so and avoid disturbing the area until clearance is given.
Declaration
No saved signature — go to Profile
By submitting I confirm this report is accurate to the best of my knowledge. I understand this report will be filed for review within 24 hours as required under WHS Act 2011.
Complete the required sections (marked *) to enable submit.

Recent awards received

🏆
Monthly prizes
Gold tier workers go into the monthly prize draw. Points reset each month — stay consistent!
How points are awarded
Points are awarded two ways — automatically when you complete compliance tasks, and by your supervisor for standout safety behaviour.
Automatic — every action
Sign-ins and pre-starts are mandatory — consistency is rewarded through the weekly streak instead.
Sign toolbox talk15 pts
Report a hazard25 pts
Weekly streak bonuses — Friday
Awarded automatically on Friday if you've hit the target every day that week.
Signed in every day Mon–Fri+25 pts
All pre-starts done Mon–Fri+25 pts
All toolbox talks signed Mon–Fri+25 pts
Supervisor awarded — discretionary
Correct PPE worn without being asked10–50
Hazard spotted and reported25–50
Great housekeeping at end of shift10–25
Helped a new worker or visitor25
Above & beyond safety behaviour50–100
Positive attitude and team culture10–25
Tier thresholds
🥇 Gold500+ points
🥈 Silver250–499 points
🥉 Bronze1–249 points

These documents are stored in your Slán Safety portal and SharePoint. Use this screen to track expiry dates and get alerts before they lapse. Expired insurance means you cannot legally work on most principal contractor sites.

WHS Compliance Certificate
Generate your monthly compliance summary to attach to tenders, prequalification applications, or send to a principal contractor on request.
Take 5

Take 5 Pre-Task Check

Stop and think before you start. 5 questions — 2 minutes.

Complete this before starting any task. If you answer NO to any question, stop work and speak to your supervisor before proceeding.
1
Have I identified all hazards associated with this task?
2
Do I have the right controls in place to manage those hazards?
3
Do I have the right tools, equipment and PPE for this task?
4
Is the area I am working in safe — no uncontrolled hazards from other trades?
5
Am I fit and capable of doing this task safely right now?
STOP WORK
You have answered NO to one or more questions. Do not proceed with this task. Notify your supervisor immediately before commencing any work in this area.
Your Take 5 will be saved and time-stamped. This record is stored in the Slán Safety system as evidence of your pre-task assessment.
⚠ EMERGENCY MUSTER
Mark every person on site as SAFE or MISSING. Do not leave site until all persons are accounted for.
0
On site
0
Accounted for
0
Unaccounted
Tap each worker to mark their status

Contact Slán Safety

Send a message to your WHS provider

Messages are reviewed during business hours. For on-site emergencies call 000. For urgent WHS issues contact your site supervisor first.

SDS Lookup

Safety Data Sheets

Search for any chemical or product used on this site

🧪
Search for a chemical
Type the product name above to find its Safety Data Sheet. All SDS documents are stored in your company SharePoint — the developer will connect this to your live chemical register.
Chemical not listed?
If a chemical is being used on site that is not in this register, it must be reported immediately. Do not use an unregistered chemical without a current SDS.
Reported - supervisor has been notified
Under WHS Regulations, a current SDS must be accessible for every hazardous chemical on site. SDS must not be older than 5 years from issue. 10 chemicals registered — SLSREG-005 v1.3. 1 expired, 1 expiring in 14 days.
Rigging & Electrical

Rigging & Electrical

Update inspection records for rigging gear and electrical equipment

Current register status
Loading register...
Submit an update
Update type
Item details
Inspection result
Notes (optional)
This update will be saved to Firebase and will feed into your SharePoint rigging & electrical register via the developer integration. A notification will be sent to the Slán Safety portal.
Rigging gear must be inspected before each use and by a licensed rigger every 3 months (AS 4991). Electrical equipment must be tested and tagged every 3 months on construction sites (AS/NZS 3760). Failed items must be removed from service immediately.

Worker-submitted Hot Works permits will appear here for your review.
Review Permit
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If equipment is missing or setup is wrong, REJECT instead with the reason — workers should fix issues before a fresh permit.
Quick-pick conditions:
Use buddy system
Extra 5m clearance
Extended fire watch (60 min)
Hourly check-in
Stop work if wind >25 km/h
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
This permit is currently active. Revoking will immediately invalidate it and notify the worker to stop work.
Reason for revocation *
Review Heights Permit
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If equipment is missing or rescue plan is not in place, REJECT instead with the reason — workers should fix issues before a fresh permit.
Quick-pick conditions:
Stop if wind >25 km/h
Dedicated spotter
100% tie-off
Hourly check-in
Brief rescue plan
Monitor exclusion zone
Beat forecast weather
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved permit — with conditions. You must acknowledge each condition before work can commence.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.
Review Confined Space
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during entry. If atmospheric levels are bad or the rescue plan isn't in place, REJECT instead — entry shouldn't go ahead until fixed.
Quick-pick conditions:
Continuous monitoring
Re-test every 30 min
SCBA required
Max 2 entrants
Hourly check-in
Rescue on standby
Evacuate on alarm
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your confined space permit with conditions. You must acknowledge each condition before entry can commence.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.

Confined Space Permit

Confined Space Entry Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Confined Space Details *
Atmospheric Test Results *
Test before entry. Acceptable: O₂ 19.5–23.5%, LEL <5%, H₂S <10 ppm, CO <30 ppm.
Acceptable: O₂ 19.5–23.5%, LEL below 5%, H₂S below 10 ppm, CO below 30 ppm. Outside any of these — STOP and evacuate.
%
%
ppm
ppm
Ventilation & Communication *
Attendant & Rescue *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS 2865 — Confined spaces · WHS Act s.38 (VIC: OHS Act 2004 s.37) — notifiable incidents
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Entry Times *
Maximum 12 hours per AS 2865 (one-shift max). End time will be capped automatically.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Atmospheric test completed and within acceptable limits
Gas detector calibrated and bump-tested today
Ventilation in place and operating
All energy sources isolated, locked out and tagged
Attendant is on station and will not leave entry point
Communication method tested between attendant and entrant
Rescue plan briefed and rescue equipment in place
Emergency contacts confirmed and reachable
PPE confirmed (harness, lifeline, respiratory if required)
Permit displayed at entry point
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices for this entry.
Close Confined Space Permit
Closeout declaration
Confirm each item below before closing. All items must be ticked.
Work has been completed
All entrants have safely exited the confined space
Final atmospheric reading taken on exit
Ventilation continued post-work where required
Area secured / entry point closed
Energy isolations re-engaged where applicable

Excavation Permit

Excavation Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Excavation Details *
Excavations 1.5m or deeper are classified as High Risk Construction Work under WHS Regulations. SWMS, shoring/benching/battering, and competent-person inspection are mandatory.
m
m
m
Soil & Support *
Australian minimum: 0.9–1.0m back from edge. For deeper excavations the spoil should be set back at least 0.6m for every 1m of depth (engineering rule for surcharge load). Loose spoil near the edge can hide tension cracks and add weight that triggers collapse.
m
Minimum: 1.0 m. Required minimum updates with depth.
Underground Services *
Strike on a live service can kill. Confirm BYDA search is current and services located on site before digging.
📞 Before You Dig Australia (BYDA)
Free national service for utility location plans (formerly Dial Before You Dig).
Phone: 1100  ·  Online: byda.com.au
Lodge enquiry at least 1 business day before excavation (the earlier the better). Plans expire 30 days after issue — re-lodge if work delayed.
Atmospheric Risk Assessment
Excavations are not automatically confined spaces — but if a hazardous atmosphere is reasonably foreseeable, the excavation must be treated as a confined space under WHS Regulation 5. Tick any that apply:
Near sewer, drain, septic, or other confined infrastructure
Near gas main / fuel line / hydrocarbon-contaminated ground
Decomposing organic matter / landfill / known contaminated soil
Plant / generator exhaust operating near excavation edge
Deep narrow trench with limited natural ventilation
None of the above — no atmospheric risk identified
⚠ Atmospheric risk identified — this excavation must be treated as a Confined Space. A separate Confined Space Permit is required, with continuous gas monitoring (O₂, LEL, H₂S, CO) before and during entry.
Access & Emergency *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
Before You Dig Australia (BYDA): 1100 · byda.com.au
WHS Reg s.305 — Excavation work · WHS Act s.38 (VIC: OHS Act 2004 s.37) — notifiable incidents
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift. End time will be capped automatically.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
DBYD search valid (under 28 days old) and reviewed
Services located on site (potholing / hydro vac where required)
Atmospheric test completed (depths over 1.5m or contaminated soil)
Shoring / support installed and inspected (depths over 1.5m)
Edge protection / guardrails in place where workers can fall in
Exclusion zone established and barricaded
Warning signage in place
Spotter assigned for plant operations near excavation
PPE confirmed (high-vis, hard hat, steel-cap boots)
Emergency response plan briefed to all workers
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices for this excavation.
Review Excavation
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during excavation. If services aren't located or shoring isn't in place at depth, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick conditions:
Hydro-vac potholing
Engineer sign-off >1.5m
Daily shoring inspection
Spotter mandatory
Stop on water ingress
CS permit if depth/atmo
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your excavation permit with conditions.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.
Close Excavation Permit
Tick all 6 to close this permit:
Work has been completed
Excavation backfilled OR secured (covers, fencing, signage)
Shoring / support removed (or left in place per design)
Edge protection / barricades removed or left securely in place
Spoil cleared or stockpiled in marked area
No services damaged. Any strikes reported.

Demolition Permit

For all demolition work — soft strip, structural, slab break-up

Permit Identification
Applicant
Demolition Details *
Hazardous Materials Assessment *
Demolition often disturbs asbestos, lead paint, silica dust, and other hazardous materials. Confirm assessment before any work commences.
Services & Adjacent Structures *
Emergency & Rescue Plan *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS 2601 — Demolition of structures · WHS Act s.38 (VIC: OHS Act 2004 s.37) — notifiable incidents
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift. End time will be capped automatically.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Asbestos register reviewed and clearance obtained
Services isolated (power, gas, water) and tagged
Structural engineer sign-off (load-bearing demolition)
Exclusion zone established and barricaded
Dust control measures in place (water suppression)
Spotter assigned and PPE confirmed
Plant operator competent and visibility clear
Adjacent structures protected
Waste removal/skip area designated
Emergency response plan briefed
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices.
Review Demolition
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If safety controls aren't in place, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick conditions:
Asbestos consultant on standby
Hourly dust monitoring required
Continuous water suppression
Stop work in winds over 30 km/h
Spotter mandatory at all times
Adjacent property pre-condition survey
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your permit with conditions.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.

Demolition complete
Site cleared of debris
Exclusion zone removed only when safe
Waste disposed correctly
No structural damage to adjacent buildings

Lifting / Crane Permit

Lifting / Crane Permit

For all lifting operations using cranes or mobile lifts

Permit Identification
Applicant
Lifting / Crane Details *
Lift Plan & Load *
Routine lifts are below 75% of crane SWL. 75–90% is complex (extra controls). Above 90% is critical and may need an engineered lift study.
t
m
m
Crew & Rigging *
Weather & Site Conditions *
km/h
Emergency & Rescue Plan *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS 2550 — Cranes, hoists & winches · AS 1418 — Cranes general requirements · WHS Act s.38 (VIC: OHS Act 2004 s.37) — notifiable incidents
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift. End time will be capped automatically.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Lift plan reviewed and approved
Rigger and dogger licences current
Crane log book and pre-start completed
Slings/chains/shackles inspected and certified
Load weight confirmed and within crane SWL
Ground bearing capacity confirmed (outriggers/pads)
Exclusion zone established below load
Communication method tested (radio/hand signals)
Wind speed checked against crane manufacturer load chart
No persons under suspended load
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices.

Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If safety controls aren't in place, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick conditions:
Lift supervisor required at all times
Cease lifts in wind above 30 km/h
Tag lines mandatory
Spotter at landing area
Hold lift if visibility reduced
Independent ground assessment required
Custom condition (optional):

Concrete Pour Permit

Concrete Pour Permit

For concrete pours — slab, column, wall, footing

Permit Identification
Applicant
Concrete Pour Details *
Mix Design & Volume *
MPa
mm
m³
Pour Method & Equipment *
Weather & Site Conditions *
°C
km/h
Emergency & Rescue Plan *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS 3610 — Formwork for concrete · AS 3600 — Concrete structures
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift. End time will be capped automatically.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Pour design and reinforcement signed off by engineer
Formwork inspected and certified
Reinforcement inspected and tied correctly
Embedments checked (services, anchor bolts, dowels)
Concrete supplier confirmed with mix design
Pour sequence and crew briefed
Pumping equipment / chute checked
Vibrators and finishing tools available
Weather forecast checked (rain, heat, frost)
Curing plan in place
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices.
Review Concrete Pour
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If safety controls aren't in place, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick conditions:
Engineer attendance required during pour
Cease pour in heavy rain
Cold-weather curing required
Hot-weather mix admixtures required
Hourly slump tests
Vibration limit checked
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your permit with conditions.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.
Close Concrete Pour Permit
Tick all to close this permit:
Pour complete to specified level
Concrete finished as per spec
Curing applied (curing compound / wet covers / blankets)
Equipment cleaned and stored
Wash-out disposed correctly
Test cylinders taken and labelled

Penetration Permit

Penetration Permit

For drilling, sawing or cutting penetrations through structure

Permit Identification
Applicant
Penetration Details *
Penetration Specifications *
Service Detection & Structural *
Striking a service or post-tensioned cable can be fatal. Confirm scan and engineer review before any drilling.
Fire Boundary & Dust Control *
Emergency & Rescue Plan *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
Before You Dig Australia (BYDA): 1100 · byda.com.au
AS/NZS 3000 — Wiring rules (concealed services)
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift. End time will be capped automatically.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Scan for services done (GPR / x-ray) and reviewed
Engineer approval for structural penetration
Service location markings visible at penetration site
Below-ceiling area cleared and protected (suspended slabs)
Spotter below for through-floor work
Dust extraction/water cooling in place
Fire/smoke risk reviewed (no penetration through fire boundary without sign-off)
Plant interlocks tested
PPE confirmed (respiratory, eye, hearing)
Patch / make-good plan in place
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices.
Review Penetration
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If safety controls aren't in place, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick conditions:
Scan reviewed by engineer before drillin
Spotter mandatory for through-slab work
Wet-cut only (no dry-cut)
Halt if service strike suspected
Fire engineer sign-off for fire wall
Make-good within 24 hours
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your permit with conditions.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.
Close Penetration Permit
Tick all to close this permit:
Penetration complete to size and location
No services struck
Dust/slurry cleaned up
Below-side area cleared
Penetration patched or labelled for follow-up
Equipment shut down and stored

Electrical Permit

Electrical Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Work Details *
Isolation & Lock Out / Tag Out *
For de-energised work: confirm isolation, lock out, and prove dead. For live work: justify why work cannot be done de-energised.
PPE & Safety Observer *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS/NZS 3000 — Wiring rules · AS/NZS 4836 — Live work
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Worker holds current electrical licence appropriate for the work
SWMS / JSA reviewed for this task
Isolation applied at correct upstream point
LOTO applied with personal lock and tag
Tested for dead at point of work using calibrated tester
Tester proven on known live source before and after
Earthing applied where required (HV / cable work)
Exclusion zone established and signposted
Electrical PPE confirmed and inspected
Safety observer in place (live work) and emergency plan briefed
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct, my licence is current, and I will follow all safe-work practices for this electrical work.
Review Electrical
Conditions for approval
If isolation isn't proven dead or licence is invalid, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick conditions:
Observer required
Re-test every 30 min
HV by auth. operator
Earthing required
Signage required
Stop on fault
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your electrical permit with conditions.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.
Close Electrical Permit
Tick all 6 to close this permit:
Work has been completed
All tools and equipment removed from work area
Earthing removed (if applied)
Personal locks and tags removed by owner
Re-energisation completed and tested OK
Work area secured and exclusion zone removed

Asbestos Permit

Asbestos Permit

Asbestos handling, removal, encapsulation, surveys

Permit Identification
Applicant
Asbestos Details *
Asbestos Type & Method *
Class A: required for friable asbestos and any quantity of asbestos-containing material. Class B: only for bonded (non-friable) asbestos under 10 m². When unsure — Class A.
Air Monitoring & Contractor *
Waste & Decontamination *
Emergency & Rescue Plan *
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
Poisons Information: 13 11 26
WHS Reg Ch 8 — Asbestos · Code of Practice: How to manage and control asbestos in the workplace · WHS Act s.38 (VIC: OHS Act 2004 s.37) — notifiable incidents
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per shift.
Pre-start Safety Checks *
SafeWork notification submitted (5 days minimum for Class A)
Licensed asbestos removalist verified (Class A or B)
Asbestos register reviewed and matches site condition
Air monitoring contractor on site and instruments calibrated
Decontamination unit operational (Class A) or wash station (Class B)
Exclusion zone signage installed at all entry points
PPE confirmed (P3 respirator, Type 5 disposable suits, gloves, boot covers)
Waste skip / sealed bags ready and labelled
Emergency contacts and clearance certificate plan in place
Workers trained and current in asbestos handling
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices.
Review Asbestos
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules. If safety controls aren't in place, REJECT instead.
Quick-pick:
Inspector required
Hourly check-in
Stop on env change
Spotter mandatory
Custom condition:
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved your permit with conditions.
I have read and accept all conditions above.
Close Asbestos Permit
Tick all to close this permit:
Asbestos work completed
Air monitoring clearance received
Decontamination unit shut down per protocol
All waste sealed, labelled, and removed
Asbestos register updated
Clearance certificate issued

Traffic Guidance Scheme (TGS) reviewed and approved

Cones / barriers measured and spaced correctly

Communications tested between TCs (radio / handsignals)
Emergency vehicle access route maintained
Reverse alarms operational on all plant

Cannot Sign Out
You have active Hot Works permits
You must close each active permit before signing out of site. Tap a permit below to complete its closeout declaration.
Close Permit
Closeout checks
Comments (optional)
Fire Watch Handover
⚠ Why this matters
AS 1674.1 requires the fire watch must continue for the full duration after work ends. If you need to leave site before it's complete, a competent person on site must take over. Their name is logged on this permit.
Hand over to *
⚠ No supervisors are currently signed in to site. You cannot hand over right now — call your supervisor to come on site, or wait for the fire watch to complete.
Photo of cooled work area *
Show the area where hot works took place. This proves no smouldering at handover.
I confirm the work area is cooled, no smouldering observed, and fire extinguisher remains in place. The named supervisor will be asked to accept this handover on their device before I can sign out.

Hot Works
Hot Works Permit

Hot Works Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Work Details
Work Times
Maximum 12 hours per AS 1674.1 (one-shift max). End time will be capped automatically.
Weather Conditions
Emergency Contacts
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS 1674.1 — Hot work on equipment containing flammables
Pre-Start Safety Checks
All items must be confirmed before the permit can be submitted.
Combustible materials removed or protected within 10m
Floor / ground swept clean of dust and debris
Drains, openings and penetrations covered
Gas lines and flammable services isolated (if applicable)
Fire extinguisher on site, in date, within 5m
Fire blanket available (where required)
PPE confirmed — gloves, helmet, eye / face protection
Ventilation adequate for the task
Adjacent workers notified
Gas cylinders secured upright and >3m from ignition source (if applicable)
Fire Watch
Fire watch required for this task
Required by AS 1674.1. Minimum 30 minutes after work in standard environments. Use 60+ minutes near timber, dust, oils, or other high-risk surroundings.
Emergency
Photo evidence

Take photos of the work area and setup. Photos optional for now (camera issue being fixed). You can submit without them.

Confirmation & Sign-off
I confirm all information is accurate and I have read the site SWMS for this task

Heights Permit

Working at Heights Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Work Details *
m
A permit is required for any work over 2m where a fall could occur.
Fall Prevention & Equipment *
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per one-shift safe-work practice. End time will be capped automatically.
Conditions & Site Setup *
Stop work if wind exceeds 30 km/h (or as per equipment manufacturer's limit).
📞 Emergency Contacts
Emergency: 000
AS/NZS 1891 — Industrial fall-arrest systems · WHS Act s.38 (VIC: OHS Act 2004 s.37) — notifiable incidents
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Anchor points rated, certified and pre-use inspected
Harness in date, undamaged, fitted correctly
Lanyard / SRL in date, free running, no damage
Edge protection / guardrails in place where required
Exclusion zone below set up with barriers and signage
Weather acceptable for the task (wind, rain, lightning assessed)
Rescue plan in place and communicated to all involved
Spotter / observer assigned (especially if working alone)
Tools tethered or area below cleared of personnel
Working surface inspected (no holes, fragile material identified)
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off

Override TFB & Approve
Total Fire Ban active
Hot works can only proceed under a valid Fire Service TFB Permit. Complete all fields below to approve with override.
Fire Service authorisation
Additional TFB-day safety measures
Beyond standard extinguisher — e.g. water source within 10m, additional extinguishers, fire hose connected, fire blanket
⚠ Override will be recorded on the permit audit trail with your name, signature, and timestamp. Worker must acknowledge all extra TFB conditions before starting work.
Total Fire Ban Active
Hot works are not permitted in your district today (State Fire Service).
Your permit will be submitted to your supervisor with a TFB flag.
🔥 TFB Test Toggle
Simulate a Total Fire Ban for testing. Set to "Force TFB" then submit a Hot Works permit to see the block.
Currently: auto
